headline: AutoZone CEO raises alarm on shift in customer behavior
summary: AutoZone (AZO) did almost everything investors typically reward, but the market sold it off anyway. The auto-parts retailer reported its strongest sales growth in over three years on Tuesday, then watched its shares drop roughly 9% to 11% by midday. The numbers were good, guidance carried a ...

headline: More Drivers Are Joining the High Miles Club. This Stock Could Pull Ahead.
summary: Sales for AutoZone have expanded from $12.6 billion in the fiscal year ended in August 2020 to an estimated $20.5 billion this fiscal year.

headline: The Most Intriguing Market Dynamic
summary: U.S. equities are at historic valuation extremes, surpassing the late-1990s tech bubble, while consumer sentiment is at all-time lows. Read full analysis here.
